rt1-5-1.title: Hands-on: cut the dose with time, distance, shielding
rt1-5-1.intro: Work the three exposure-control levers on a live 80 Ci Ir-192 bay. Prove the inverse-square law by stepping back, stack half-value layers until the boundary post reads under 2 mR/h, then plan the job time so the dose stays ALARA.

tasks.label: Double your distance and cut the rate to one quarter
tasks.hint: I1d1^2 = I2d2^2 - drag the worker marker out to twice the start distance with no slabs stacked.

tasks.label: Stack lead HVL slabs until the boundary reads under 2 mR/h
tasks.hint: Each slab is one half-value layer (about 4.8 mm lead for Ir-192) and halves the rate - watch the boundary post readout.

tasks.label: Plan at least 10 min of work under a 20 mrem budget
tasks.hint: Dose = rate x hours. If 10 minutes busts the budget, lower the rate first with distance or shielding, then set the time slider.

rt1-5-2.title: Hands-on: survey and post the radiography boundary
rt1-5-2.intro: Your survey meter is your only sense for radiation around a shielded exposure device. Carry it to each boundary stake and wait for a stable reading before recording - then move the over-limit stake until the posted line truly reads 2 mR/h.

tasks.label: Record a stable meter reading at stakes A, B, and C
tasks.hint: Hold the meter still at each stake until the needle settles - Record rejects a reading that is still moving, just like a real ion chamber.

tasks.label: Re-post stake B where the meter reads the 2 mR/h limit
tasks.hint: Drag stake B outward along its radial and watch the rate fall by inverse square; release when it sits at the posting limit.
